| MCP:dPjUR5 | # SpringBoot Java Template | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | [![Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/url?style=social&url=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fgeminikims)](https://twitter.com/geminikims) | MCP:dPjUR5 | [![Youtube](https://img.shields.io/youtube/channel/views/UCDh8zEDofOcrOMAOnSVL9Tg?label=Youtube&style=social)](https://www.youtube.com/@geminikims) | MCP:dPjUR5 | [![CI](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml) | MCP:dPjUR5 | [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-green.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | This is not the best structure. This is a good basic structure to use early in the project when productivity is important. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | Remember, as your software grows, your structure must grow too. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | # **Modules** | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## Core | MCP:dPjUR5 | Each submodule of this module is responsible for one domain service. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | This must make the modular structure grow with the growth of the service. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ### core:core-api | MCP:dPjUR5 | It is the only executable module in the project. It is structured to have domains to maximize initial development productivity. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | It is also responsible for providing APIs and setting up frameworks for services. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ### core:core-enum | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | This module contains enums that are used by `core-api` and must be delivered to external modules. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 |
| MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## Clients | MCP:dPjUR5 | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with external systems. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ### clients:clients-example | MCP:dPjUR5 | This module shows an example of HTTP communication with `Spring-Cloud-Open-Feign`. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 |
| MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## Storage | MCP:dPjUR5 | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with the various storages. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ### storage:db-core | MCP:dPjUR5 | This module shows an example of connecting to `MySql` using `Spring-Data-JPA`. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 |
| MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## Support | MCP:dPjUR5 | Submodules of this module are responsible for additional support. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ### support:logging | MCP:dPjUR5 | This module supports logging of service and has a dependency added for distributed tracing support. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | It also includes dependencies to support `Sentry`. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ### support:monitoring | MCP:dPjUR5 | This module supports monitoring of services. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 |
| MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## Tests | MCP:dPjUR5 | Submodules of this module are responsible for the convenience of writing test codes. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ### tests:api-docs | MCP:dPjUR5 | This module is for writing spring-rest-docs conveniently. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 |
| MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | # Dependency Management | MCP:dPjUR5 | All dependency versioning is done through `gradle.properties` file. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | If you want to add a new dependency, put the version in `gradle.properties` and load it in `build.gradle`. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 |
| MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | # Runtime Profiles | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## local | MCP:dPjUR5 | This profile aims to configure an environment that can be developed even if the network is disconnected. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## local-dev | MCP:dPjUR5 | This profile aims configurations that allow me to connect to the DEV environment from my local machine. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## dev | MCP:dPjUR5 | This profile exists for deploying Development environments. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## staging | MCP:dPjUR5 | This profile exists for deploying Staging environments. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## live | MCP:dPjUR5 | This profile exists for deploying Live environments. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 |
| MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | # Test Tasks & Tags | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## test | MCP:dPjUR5 | This is a collection of test-tasks that we want to run on `CI`. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | If you want to change the settings, modify the `build.gradle` file. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## unitTest | MCP:dPjUR5 | This is a group of tests that typically have no dependencies, are fast to run, and test a single feature. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## contextTest | MCP:dPjUR5 | This is a task that runs with SpringContext and has integration tests. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## restDocsTest | MCP:dPjUR5 | This is a task to create asciidoc based on spring-rest-docs. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## developTest | MCP:dPjUR5 | This is a task of tests that should not be run in `CI`. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | This is a good tag to use if you're not good at writing tests. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 |
| MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | # Recommended Preferences | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## Git Hook | MCP:dPjUR5 | This setting makes run `lint` on every commit. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ``` | MCP:dPjUR5 | $ git config core.hookspath .githooks | MCP:dPjUR5 | ``` | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ## IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:dPjUR5 | This setting makes it easier to run the `test code` out of the box. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | ``` | MCP:dPjUR5 | // Gradle Build and run with IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:dPjUR5 | Build, Execution, Deployment > Build Tools > Gradle > Run tests using > IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:dPjUR5 | ``` | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | If you want to apply lint settings to the format of IDEA, please refer to the guide below. | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | [Spring Java Format IntelliJ IDEA](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat#intellij-idea) | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | --- | MCP:dPjUR5 | | MCP:dPjUR5 | # Supported By | MCP:dPjUR5 |
| MCP:dPjUR5 | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: e39e158f2cced5a68cc724fd8a0d51b0f066d064)

| MCP:dPjUR5 | name: CI
| MCP:dPjUR5 | 
| MCP:dPjUR5 | on:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |   push:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |     branches: [ "main" ]
| MCP:dPjUR5 |   pull_request:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |     branches: [ "main" ]
| MCP:dPjUR5 | 
| MCP:dPjUR5 | permissions:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |   contents: read
| MCP:dPjUR5 | 
| MCP:dPjUR5 | jobs:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |   build:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
| MCP:dPjUR5 | 
| MCP:dPjUR5 |     steps:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |       - uses: actions/checkout@v3
| MCP:dPjUR5 |       - name: Set up JDK
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         uses: actions/setup-java@v3
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         with:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |           java-version: '21'
| MCP:dPjUR5 |           distribution: 'adopt'
| MCP:dPjUR5 |       - name: Lint
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         uses: gradle/gradle-build-action@67421db6bd0bf253fb4bd25b31ebb98943c375e1
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         with:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |           arguments: checkFormat
| MCP:dPjUR5 |       - name: Test
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         uses: gradle/gradle-build-action@67421db6bd0bf253fb4bd25b31ebb98943c375e1
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         with:
| MCP:dPjUR5 |           arguments: test

| MCP:dPjUR5 | pluginManagement {
| MCP:dPjUR5 |     plugins {
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         id 'org.springframework.boot' version "${springBootVersion}"
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         id 'io.spring.dependency-management' version "${springDependencyManagementVersion}"
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         id 'org.asciidoctor.jvm.convert' version "${asciidoctorConvertVersion}"
| MCP:dPjUR5 |         id 'io.spring.javaformat' version "${springJavaFormatVersion}"
| MCP:dPjUR5 |     }
| MCP:dPjUR5 | }
| MCP:dPjUR5 | 
| MCP:dPjUR5 | rootProject.name = 'spring-boot-java-template'
| MCP:dPjUR5 | 
| MCP:dPjUR5 | include 'core:core-enum'
| MCP:dPjUR5 | include 'core:core-api'
| MCP:dPjUR5 | include 'storage:db-core'
| MCP:dPjUR5 | include 'tests:api-docs'
| MCP:dPjUR5 | include 'support:logging'
| MCP:dPjUR5 | include 'support:monitoring'
| MCP:dPjUR5 | include 'clients:client-example'

data.content:
Here's how testing is organized in this Spring Boot Java template:

---

## Named Test Tasks (Gradle Testing Suites)

The project uses **Gradle Testing Suites** (defined in `build.gradle` under `subprojects`) to create four distinct, named test tasks, each targeting a specific JUnit 5 tag:

| Task Name | Included Tag(s) | Excluded Tag(s) | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| **`unitTest`** | *(all except the excluded ones)* | `develop`, `context`, `restdocs` | Fast, isolated tests with no Spring context or external dependencies. |
| **`contextTest`** | `context` | — | Integration tests that load a Spring application context. |
| **`restDocsTest`** | `restdocs` | — | Tests that generate AsciiDoc API documentation via Spring REST Docs. |
| **`developTest`** | `develop` | — | Tests that are **only meant for local development**, never run in CI. |

Each task is placed in the `verification` group so they appear alongside standard Gradle lifecycle tasks.

---

## Excluded Tags

The base `test` task (the default JUnit Platform task) is configured to **exclude two tags**:

```groovy
useJUnitPlatform {
    excludeTags 'develop', 'restdocs'
}
```

- **`develop`** — Excluded because these are personal/scratch tests not intended for automated pipelines.
- **`restdocs`** — Excluded because REST Docs tests generate documentation artifacts rather than validating behavior; they are a separate concern.

The `unitTest` target adds a third exclusion (`context`) to ensure only truly fast, context-free unit tests run there.

---

## CI-Oriented vs. Develop-Only Test Execution

### CI Pipeline (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

The GitHub Actions workflow runs on pushes and PRs to `main` and executes:

1. **`checkFormat`** — Code style / lint check.
2. **`test`** — The default `test` task, which **excludes** the `develop` and `restdocs` tags.

This means CI runs **unit tests**, **context tests**, and all other un-tagged tests, but deliberately skips:
- `develop`-tagged tests (unreliable / not CI-safe).
- `restdocs`-tagged tests (documentation generation, not a gate).

### Local / Develop Execution

Developers who want to run the full suite can invoke any of the specific tasks:

```bash
./gradlew unitTest          # Fast feedback loop
./gradlew contextTest       # Integration tests
./gradlew restDocsTest      # Generate API docs
./gradlew developTest       # Experimental / scratch tests (tagged with @Tag("develop"))
```

The `developTest` task exists precisely for tests that are still in progress, are flaky, or require manual setup — things you wouldn't want blocking a CI build. As the README notes, it's "a good tag to use if you're not good at writing tests" (i.e., during early development).

### Additional Integration

The `restDocsTest` task is wired as a dependency of the `asciidoctor` task, so generating documentation automatically triggers REST Docs tests first:

```groovy
tasks.named('asciidoctor').configure {
    dependsOn restDocsTest
}
```

---

## Summary

The template creates a clean separation: **CI gets a safe, fast subset** (`test` = everything except `develop` and `restdocs`), while **developers get fine-grained tasks** (`unitTest`, `contextTest`, `restDocsTest`, `developTest`) for local use. The `develop` tag acts as an escape hatch for tests that shouldn't run in pipelines yet.
